model_tasks: determine demanded components according to planning
purchase missing parts and put on stock

merged_tasks.model: determine demanded components according to planning
purchase missing parts and put on stock

user_text:
The process begins with the receipt of a customer order. Once the order is received, the next step involves processing the customer's demand. Following this, the necessary components required to fulfill the order are determined according to a pre-existing plan.

At this point, a decision is made. If any parts are missing, they are purchased and added to the stock. If no parts are missing, the process continues without purchasing additional components. Regardless of whether parts were purchased or not, the process then moves on to production.

After production is completed, quality checks are performed on the product. Another decision is made based on the results of these quality checks. If the product passes the quality checks, the process continues. If the product does not meet the quality standards, follow-up production is performed to address any issues. Once the follow-up production is completed, the process continues.

The next step involves filling the product into appropriate packaging. After the product is packaged, it is then packed and labeled. Following this, the product is transported to the warehouse.

Finally, the product is sent to the customer, marking the end of the process.
output_text:
The process begins with receiving a customer order. Once the order is received, the next step is to process the customer's demand. This involves determining the components needed to fulfill the order according to the planning. After identifying the required components, the request is reviewed.

At this point, a decision is made. If any parts are missing, they are purchased and added to the stock. If no parts are missing, or after the missing parts have been stocked, the process continues to production.

The production phase involves manufacturing the product. Once production is complete, quality checks are performed to ensure the product meets the required standards. If the product passes the quality checks, the process moves forward. If the product does not pass, follow-up production is performed to address any issues, after which the product undergoes quality checks again.

Once the product has successfully passed the quality checks, it is filled into packaging. After packaging, the product is packed and labeled appropriately. The next step is to transport the product to the warehouse.

Finally, the product is sent to the customer, concluding the process.
